summary: Updated the vendored tools/m2c copy to upstream m2c commit c5625f0, bringing a package layout change from src to m2c, Poetry-based dependency management, Python 3.7 CI, refreshed type stubs, and many MIPS/PPC decompiler behavior updates with end-to-end test coverage. The largest technical changes in the excerpt are MIPS normalization and pattern handling for PIC $gp setup/jump-table code, IDO unrolled memcpy/struct-copy loops, PS2-style ori stack additions, hi/lo multiply/divide forms, and new fictive memcpy macros. The PR also exempted tools/calcprogress and tools/m2c paths from the repository editorconfig workflow, matching the title's skip-editorconfig intent.
what_changed: Moved the vendored m2c Python package import path from src.* to m2c.*; the launcher now imports from m2c.main and tools/m2c/m2c/__init__.py was added while tools/m2c/src/__init__.py was removed.
Updated tools/m2c to upstream commit c5625f0, per the PR body.

Introduced Poetry project metadata and lockfile for m2c, and updated the m2c GitHub workflow to install Poetry with pipx, use Python 3.7, cache Poetry dependencies, run tests via poetry run, run mypy via poetry run, and run black via poetry run.
Changed m2c README installation and test-generation documentation from Python 3.6/sm64tools assumptions to Python 3.7, spimdisasm, Poetry installation, and PowerPC MWCC/WINE requirements.
Excluded tools/calcprogress and tools/m2c paths from the repository editorconfig workflow by filtering changed files with grep -vE.

Added MIPS assembly normalization/patterns for stripping PIC .cpload-style $gp setup at function starts, removing conservative $gp additions before jr jump-table dispatch, normalizing small ori $sp,N forms to addiu, and recognizing IDO unrolled memcpy loops used for struct copies.
Added fictive MIPS memcpy instructions mapped to C macro calls M2C_MEMCPY_ALIGNED and M2C_MEMCPY_UNALIGNED.

Adjusted MIPS hi/lo handling so mult/multu/dmult/dmultu/madd/maddu are normalized into an explicit destination-register form and div/divu-style operations use a three-argument representation; mtlo and mthi evaluation support was added.
Expanded MIPS register modeling by including $zero in all_regs.

Added or refreshed many end-to-end tests, notably struct-copy, got, incbin, delay-slot-branch, phi-samevalue-replacement, ps2, and prevent-uses-for-stack-spills cases, plus updated expected outputs for existing tests.
Updated pycparser and capstone .pyi stubs and m2c test helper scripts.
smart_moves: The update was imported as a broad upstream sync rather than a piecemeal local patch, making the source of the tooling behavior clear: upstream m2c commit c5625f0.
The editorconfig workflow was explicitly taught to ignore vendored tool directories, reducing noisy formatting churn when syncing third-party tooling.

The MIPS memcpy/struct-copy support uses assembly-pattern replacement into fictive instructions and then emits explicit M2C_MEMCPY_ALIGNED/M2C_MEMCPY_UNALIGNED macros, preserving decompiler intent without pretending the original assembly was a direct libc memcpy call.
PIC $gp setup and $gp jump-table additions are stripped only through targeted patterns; the GpJumpPattern comment notes a conservative scope rather than rewriting all $gp additions.
The memcpy pattern derives copy length by walking backward to the addiu that computes the end pointer, rather than hardcoding one exact loop shape.
The update pairs backend pattern changes with end-to-end tests and expected output updates, which is important for a decompilation helper where regressions can be subtle.
Moving to Poetry and a lockfile makes local m2c runs, CI, typing, and formatting checks more reproducible.
decomp_lessons: Vendored decompilation tools should be syncable from upstream with minimal local formatting interference; CI/editorconfig filters can be necessary for third-party directories.

When an assembly idiom represents compiler-generated helper behavior, such as unrolled struct-copy loops, it can be better to normalize it into a fictive operation/macro than to emit a long and misleading sequence of scalar loads and stores.

Pattern-based normalization should document architecture and compiler assumptions. This update distinguishes MIPS1 versus MIPS2 memcpy loop shapes, aligned versus unaligned copies, PIC $gp setup, jump-table $gp additions, and PS2-specific forms.

For MIPS matching, model special registers and instruction pseudo-forms explicitly. The update adds $zero to all_regs and represents hi/lo-producing operations with explicit argument structure so later analysis can reason about inputs, outputs, and clobbers.

End-to-end tests are useful as executable documentation for translator behavior; new cases like struct-copy, got, incbin, ps2, and stack-spill prevention show what idioms the tool is expected to tolerate.
Do not assume old project docs are accurate after a tooling sync: update compiler/disassembler dependencies, package installation, and environment variables alongside code.
naming_conventions: MIPS synthetic memcpy operations are named with a .fictive suffix: memcpy.aligned.fictive and memcpy.unaligned.fictive.
C helper macros for fictive memcpy operations use the M2C_ prefix: M2C_MEMCPY_ALIGNED and M2C_MEMCPY_UNALIGNED.
New MIPS pattern classes use descriptive Pattern suffixes, such as SetGpPattern, AlignedMemcpyPattern, UnalignedMemcpyPattern, GpJumpPattern, and OriSpPattern.
The vendored Python package is named m2c rather than src, so imports use m2c.main and module paths under tools/m2c/m2c/.
End-to-end test directories use hyphenated behavior names such as struct-copy, prevent-uses-for-stack-spills, delay-slot-branch, phi-samevalue-replacement, and float-conversions.
Per-test compiler/options files follow the existing *-flags.txt convention, such as mwcc-o4p-flags.txt and manual-flags.txt.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Strip PIC .cpload-like setup only at the start of a function by matching an initial wildcard, addiu $gp,$gp,_, and addu $gp,$gp,$t9 sequence and replacing it with nop.
Recognize IDO unrolled memcpy/struct-copy loops by matching MIPS1 and MIPS2 loop bodies, with separate aligned lw/sw and unaligned lwl/lwr/swl/swr variants.
Infer memcpy length by scanning backward before the loop for the addiu that computes the end pointer, while checking whether the induction register was moved from the same source.
Handle extra trailing copied words through epilogue patterns, while preserving a TODO noting known imperfect matching around MIPS2 reordering.
Replace matched memcpy loops with addiu setup for synthetic from/to registers, a fictive memcpy instruction with length, induction-variable increments, wildcard items, and a trailing nop.
Remove addu $x,$x,$gp before jr $x for position-independent jump tables instead of globally deleting all $gp additions.
Normalize ori $x,$sp,N to addiu $x,$sp,N only for small nonnegative immediates below 16 and when the destination is not $sp.
Normalize mult/multu/dmult/dmultu/madd/maddu into forms with an explicit first destination register, using $zero where no LO copy is desired.
Add mtlo and mthi evaluation so writes to LO/HI are tracked as register state changes.
Map fictive memcpy operations to macro-like C calls rather than relying on raw load/store expression reconstruction.
review_feedback: No issue comments or review comments were present in the provided slice.
One review is recorded in the metadata, but no review body or actionable feedback is included in the excerpt.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that update tools/m2c beyond upstream commit c5625f0.
Search for uses or modifications of M2C_MEMCPY_ALIGNED and M2C_MEMCPY_UNALIGNED in later decompilation work.
Check whether the TODO in AlignedMemcpyPattern about MIPS2 epilogue matching was resolved in a later m2c sync.
Search for later changes to editorconfig exclusions for vendored tools.
Compare later m2c handling of $gp PIC setup and jump-table patterns against SetGpPattern and GpJumpPattern.
